pages.raw_text:
Operations have commenced on
the new Stevens st. bridge across
the Spokane river. When COM
pleted it will add another crossing
by which the Great Northern depot
may be reached. The steel being
used Is that which was bought for
the Havermale st. bridge, since
knocked down by high water. The
council recently authorized the
board of public works to go ahead
and put the steel in place on Ste
vents st. After it was bought and
laid down on the ground for the
purpose of bridging the river at
Havermale ST., the proposition to
build only one span on Howard st.
of reinforced concrete and elimi-
nate the Tavermale bridge came up.


The Washington st. bridge is
closed to any but foot traffic on ac-
count of high water making this
crossing dangerous to life. Street
cars now stop at either end and
transfer passengers. No teams are
allowed to cross. This necessitated
them going around on either How
ard or Division streets.


The Havermale bridge was pur-
chased last fall and left on the cars
until the city had an $500 demur-
rage bill to pay.
